comment: Tooltip definitions for technical terms. Source: ASHRAE Handbook—Refrigeration 2022 Ch. 24, IEC 60335-2-89:2019, ASHRAE 34-2022.

td.def: Air-to-coil ΔT — how much warmer the room air is than the evaporator coil surface. Lower TD = larger evaporator, gentler dehumidification, higher product RH preserved.

sst.def: Refrigerant saturation temperature at the evaporator outlet — sets the cold-side operating point. Lower SST = colder coil = larger TD = more capacity per kg refrigerant.

sct.def: Refrigerant saturation temperature at the condenser outlet — sets the hot-side operating point. For Pakistan ambient 43°C, SCT typically 55°C air-cooled.

ach.def: How many times the room air volume is replaced by ambient air through doors and gaps in 24 hours. Drives infiltration heat load.
ach.typical: Ranges from 1.4 (huge warehouse) to 38 (tiny 5 m³ box) — see ASHRAE Ch. 24 Table 5

rh.def: Water vapour content of air as a % of saturation. Inside a cold room, target RH depends on product: produce 85–95%, frozen 95%, dry storage 50–70%.

tr.def: Imperial unit of cooling capacity. 1 TR = 12 000 BTU/hr = 3.517 kW. Common in South Asia for chiller sizing.
tr.typical: 1 TR ≈ small walk-in cooler; 50 TR ≈ medium cold store

lfl.def: Minimum vol% of refrigerant in air to support flame propagation. A2L refrigerants need rooms large enough that a full leak can't reach ¼ of LFL.

awef.def: US DOE 10 CFR 431 minimum efficiency for commercial walk-in coolers/freezers. Pakistan has no equivalent regulation.

thr.def: The heat the condenser must dissipate = evaporator load + compressor work = Q_evap × (1 + 1/COP). Drives condenser sizing.

lambda.def: How easily heat passes through a material. Lower λ = better insulation. Izhar PIR uses λ = 0.020 W/m·K (aged), among the best commercial cold-store insulations.

mcwb.def: Wet bulb temperature occurring on average at the design dry bulb hour. Drives infiltration enthalpy load and evaporative condenser sizing.
mcwb.typical: Karachi 26°C, Multan 21°C — coastal cities have higher MCWB

leakable.def: Refrigerant that could escape into the conditioned room if the system leaks — = evaporator + piping inside the room (downstream of safety shut-off valves). NOT the total system charge.
leakable.typical: Smaller than total charge; smarter safety basis than legacy formulas.

cop.def: Cold output divided by electrical input. Higher COP = more efficient. R-290 (propane) typically gives 5–10% better COP than R-404A.
